title: 10 best herbal teas for bleeding gums
intro_desc: Herbal teas for bleeding gums are infusions made from plants that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate the symptoms of gum disease and promote oral health. These teas can reduce inflammation, kill harmful bacteria, and strengthen gum tissue, thereby reducing bleeding. Examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for bleeding gums include chamomile, sage, calendula, and clove. By drinking these teas regularly, individuals can improve their oral health, reduce the risk of gum disease, and avoid the discomfort and potential complications associated with bleeding gums. This can lead to improved overall health, as gum disease has been linked to various systemic conditions such as diabetes, heart disease, and respiratory infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes a gel-like consistency when mixed with water. This gel-like substance can help to soothe and protect irritated gum tissue, reducing inflammation and bleeding. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-bacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can further help to reduce gum inflammation and promote oral health. The tea is also rich in vitamin C and other antioxidants, which can support collagen production and help to maintain healthy gums. Drinking marshmallow tea regularly may help to strengthen gum tissue, reduce inflammation, and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage that forms a protective film on the gums, reducing inflammation and promoting healing, which is important for bleeding gums.
Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce gum inflammation and swelling, thereby alleviating bleeding.
Antibacterial property: Marshmallow tea contains antimicrobial agents like tannins, which help inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infections and bleeding.
Astringent property: The astringent properties of marshmallow tea, due to its tannins content, help constrict blood vessels and tighten gum tissues, which can help control bleeding.
Analgesic property: Marshmallow tea has analgesic compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help alleviate gum pain and discomfort associated with bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of dried marshmallow root and 4 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove marshmallow root pieces.
Add 1 teaspoon of powdered marshmallow root to the liquid and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, for bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help reduce gum inflammation and fight oral bacteria. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce the swelling and irritation of the gums, while its antimicrobial properties can help eliminate the bacteria that cause gum disease and lead to bleeding gums. Additionally, sage tea is rich in tannins, which can help tighten gum tissue and reduce bleeding. Drinking sage tea regularly may help promote healthy gums and prevent gum disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea reduces inflammation, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps alleviate the swelling and redness that often accompany gum infection or irritation. The active constituents responsible for sage's anti-inflammatory properties include rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Sage tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria, which is crucial for treating bleeding gums since it helps address the underlying infection or imbalance that can cause gum inflammation and bleeding. The antimicrobial properties of sage tea come from its essential oils, such as thujone, camphor, and 1,8-cineole.

Astringent: Sage tea acts as an astringent, which tightens and strengthens gum tissue, reducing bleeding and promoting healing. This astringent property is primarily due to the tannins present in sage.

Analgesic: Sage tea has pain-relieving properties, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps alleviate discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for sage's analgesic effects include various terpenes and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Sage tea contains antioxidants, which help protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage. This antioxidant property supports overall gum health and aids in the healing process. The antioxidants in sage tea include rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and various flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the sage leaves.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, then sip slowly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. Plantain tea is also rich in tannins, which can help to tighten and strengthen gum tissue, reducing bleeding. Additionally, plantain tea contains vitamins and minerals that can promote the healing of damaged gum tissue, further helping to prevent bleeding. Drinking plantain tea regularly may help to maintain good oral health and prevent gum disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea reduces inflammation, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps alleviate irritation and swelling in the gums. The active constituents responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties include flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin.
Astringent: Plantain tea has astringent properties, which help tighten and constrict tissues, reducing bleeding from gums. This is due to the presence of tannins, which are natural astringents.

Antibacterial: Plantain tea inhibits the growth of bacteria, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps control the buildup of harmful bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and disease. The antibacterial properties come from various compounds, including flavonoids, saponins, and tannins.

Antioxidant: Plantain tea contains antioxidants, which help protect gum tissues from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for bleeding gums as it supports overall gum health and helps reduce inflammation. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins.

Analgesic: Plantain tea has pain-relieving properties, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps alleviate discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for its analgesic properties include flavonoids, saponins, and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a clean, pesticide-free area.
Rinse the leaves thoroughly, then tear them into small pieces and place in a pot.
Add 4 cups of water to the pot, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the leaves, and let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1 cup of the plantain tea, three times a day, to help treat bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help tighten and strengthen gum tissues, reducing bleeding. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of comfrey tea can also help alleviate pain and inflammation in the gums. Additionally, comfrey tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, including calcium, magnesium, and vitamin C, which are essential for maintaining healthy gums and teeth. Regular consumption of comfrey tea may help improve overall oral health and prevent gum disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of allantoin and rosmarinic acid, helps reduce gum inflammation, thus alleviating bleeding.
Astringent: Comfrey tea's astringent property, caused by tannins, tightens and strengthens gum tissues, reducing bleeding.
Wound healing: Comfrey tea's wound healing property, owing to allantoin and rosmarinic acid, promotes gum tissue regeneration, decreasing bleeding.
Analgesic: Comfrey tea's analgesic property, attributed to rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids, reduces gum pain, thereby alleviating bleeding.
Antimicrobial: Comfrey tea's antimicrobial property, due to rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids, helps control gum infections, minimizing bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 fresh comfrey leaves, rinse, and chop into small pieces.
Place chopped leaves in a pot, add 2 cups of water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, simmer for 15 minutes, then let the mixture steep for an additional 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid, discarding the solids, and store the tea in the refrigerator.
Dilute the chilled tea with an equal amount of water and rinse your mouth with the solution for 30 seconds, twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight against harmful bacteria in the mouth. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can help to strengthen blood vessels and promote wound healing, which can reduce bleeding and inflammation in the gums. Drinking pot marigold tea regularly may also improve overall oral health by reducing plaque buildup and bad breath. Additionally, the tea's soothing and calming effects can help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with bleeding gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea reduces inflammation, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps alleviate irritation and swelling in the gums. This property is attributed to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids.

Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria, which is crucial for treating bleeding gums by helping to prevent and reduce gum infections. The antimicrobial effects come from active constituents such as essential oils and alkaloids.

Astringent: Pot marigold tea has astringent properties that help tighten and strengthen gum tissues, which is important for controlling bleeding and promoting gum healing. The astringent effect is primarily due to the tea's tannin content.

Antioxidant: The tea contains antioxidants that help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage, which is important for maintaining gum health and reducing the risk of gum disease. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and carotenoids.

Analgesic: Pot marigold tea has pain-relieving properties, which is important for treating bleeding gums as it helps alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with gum inflammation. The analgesic effect is due to the presence of various compounds, including flavonoids and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, place 1 teaspoon of dried pot marigold flowers.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers.
Drink the tea, up to three times daily, to help with bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and styptic properties that can help to reduce inflammation and stop bleeding. The astringent properties of yarrow help to tighten tissues, which can reduce the amount of bleeding from the gums. Additionally, yarrow has antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infection and promote healing. Drinking yarrow tea or rinsing the mouth with yarrow tea can provide relief from bleeding gums and improve oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces gum inflammation, helping to alleviate bleeding. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent property tightens and strengthens gum tissue, reducing bleeding. This is attributed to the tannins in yarrow.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial property fights oral bacteria, preventing gum infections that can cause bleeding. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils, flavonoids, and lactones.
Analgesic: Yarrow tea's analgesic property alleviates gum pain, making it easier to brush and floss, which helps stop bleeding. This property is due to the presence of alkamides and flavonoids.

Wound healing: Yarrow tea's wound healing property promotes gum tissue regeneration, helping to stop bleeding and prevent future occurrences. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids, lactones, and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow herb to a tea infuser.
Pour boiling water over the yarrow herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the yarrow tea into a cup, discarding the used herb.
Drink the warm yarrow tea up to three times a day to help with bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains certain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These compounds, such as glycyrrhizin, can help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause gum disease. Additionally, licorice tea may help to strengthen the gums and promote the growth of healthy tissue. Drinking licorice tea regularly may help to maintain healthy gums and prevent bleeding. However, it is important to note that while licorice tea can be helpful for bleeding gums, it is not a substitute for good oral hygiene practices such as brushing and flossing regularly.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory property, attributed to its glycyrrhizin and flavonoid content, helps reduce gum inflammation and irritation, which are often associated with bleeding gums.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of licorice tea, due to its glycyrrhizin and flavonoid constituents, inhibits the growth of harmful oral bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and bleeding.

Antioxidant: Licorice tea's antioxidant properties, derived from its flavonoids, help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage, promoting gum health and reducing the likelihood of bleeding.

Wound healing: The wound healing properties of licorice tea, owing to its glycyrrhizin and flavonoid content, promote tissue regeneration and help accelerate the healing of damaged gum tissues, reducing bleeding.
Analgesic: Licorice tea's analgesic property, attributed to its glycyrrhizin and flavonoid constituents, helps alleviate gum pain and discomfort, providing relief from bleeding gums.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove licorice root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down until it's warm, but not hot.
Sip the tea slowly, letting it swish around in your mouth before swallowing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce gum inflammation and fight oral bacteria. The tea's high concentration of vitamin K and calcium also promotes blood clotting and strengthens gum tissue, which can help stop bleeding. Additionally, fenugreek tea's soothing and cooling effects can provide relief from gum pain and discomfort. Regular consumption of fenugreek tea can help maintain good oral hygiene and prevent gum disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fenugreek tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces gum inflammation, helping to alleviate bleeding. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds.

Antimicrobial: Fenugreek tea's antimicrobial property inhibits the growth of periodontal pathogens, reducing gum infection and bleeding. This property is attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins.
Antioxidant: Fenugreek tea's antioxidant property reduces oxidative stress in the gums, promoting gum health and reducing bleeding. This property is due to the presence of polyphenols and flavonoids.

Wound healing: Fenugreek tea's wound healing property promotes gum tissue regeneration, reducing bleeding and improving gum health. This property is attributed to the presence of mucilage and polysaccharides.
Analgesic: Fenugreek tea's analgesic property alleviates gum pain, reducing discomfort and promoting oral health. This property is due to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 2 cups of water.
Add - Steep 1 teaspoon of dried fenugreek leaves or 1 fenugreek tea bag in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain - Pour the tea through a strainer into a cup, discarding the leaves or tea bag.
Mix - Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey or a natural sweetener, if desired.
Serve - Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb is also rich in vitamin C, which is essential for the production of collagen, a protein that helps maintain the health and structure of gum tissue. By promoting collagen synthesis and reducing inflammation, gotu kola tea can help strengthen gums and prevent bleeding. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties can help tighten and tone gum tissue, further reducing the risk of bleeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tea reduces inflammation, which is important for bleeding gums as it helps alleviate the swelling and irritation that can cause gum bleeding. This property is attributed to the triterpene saponins present in gotu kola.

Wound healing: Gotu kola tea promotes wound healing, which is crucial for bleeding gums as it aids in the repair of damaged gum tissue. The active constituents responsible for this property are asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madecassic acid.

Antimicrobial: Gotu kola tea has antimicrobial properties, which are essential for bleeding gums as it helps control the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and bleeding. The antimicrobial activity of gotu kola is attributed to its essential oil and tannins.

Antioxidant: Gotu kola tea has antioxidant properties, which are important for bleeding gums as it helps protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. The antioxidant properties of gotu kola are attributed to its phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Collagen synthesis: Gotu kola tea promotes collagen synthesis, which is vital for bleeding gums as it helps strengthen the gums and improve their elasticity. The active constituents responsible for this property are asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madecassic acid.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried gotu kola leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Drink the tea, allowing it to soothe and heal bleeding gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European red raspberry tea helps with bleeding gums because it contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can tighten and constrict tissues, reducing bleeding. The tea is also rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that support oral health. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and swelling, providing relief from bleeding gums. The tea's antibacterial properties may also help reduce the number of bacteria in the mouth, promoting better oral hygiene and health. Drinking European red raspberry tea regularly can help maintain healthy gums and prevent bleeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: European red raspberry tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, like tannins and ellagic acid, which help reduce gum inflammation and irritation, thereby alleviating bleeding gums.
Astringent: The astringent properties of raspberry tea, due to its tannin content, help tighten and strengthen gum tissues, reducing gum bleeding and promoting oral health.
Antioxidant: Rich in antioxidants, such as anthocyanins and vitamin C, raspberry tea helps protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage, supporting gum health and minimizing bleeding.

Antibacterial: European red raspberry tea exhibits antibacterial properties, mainly from its ellagitannin content, that help control oral bacterial growth, preventing gum infections and subsequent gum bleeding.

Vitamin C source: European red raspberry tea is a good source of vitamin C, which is essential for collagen production and gum tissue strength. Adequate vitamin C intake helps maintain healthy gums and minimizes bleeding.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried European red raspberry leaves to the boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Drink the tea up to three times a day, as needed, for bleeding gums.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bleeding gums with herbal teas is by using a mouthwash prepared from natural herbs like chamomile, sage, or calendula. Steep the herbs in hot water for 10-15 minutes, then strain and cool the liquid. Rinse your mouth with this herbal tea twice a day, gently swishing it around your gums. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation, strengthen gum tissue, and control bleeding. Regular use can promote oral health and improve overall gum condition.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bleeding gums that are treated with herbal teas include gingivitis and periodontal disease. Herbal teas like green tea and chamomile tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and bleeding. Gingivitis, an early stage of gum disease, can be managed with herbal teas that contain antibacterial agents, such as cloves and goldenseal, which can help reduce plaque buildup and fight off infections. Additionally, myrrh and calendula teas have been used to treat mouth ulcers and sores, providing relief from pain and promoting healing. However, it is important to consult with a dental professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
